commands.raw_help:
root@kali:~# opentaxii-add-api-root -h
 usage: opentaxii-add-api-root [-h] -t TITLE [-d DESCRIPTION] [--default]
 
 Add a new taxii2 ApiRoot object.
 
 options:
 -h, --help            show this help message and exit
 -t, --title TITLE     Title of the api root
 -d, --description DESCRIPTION
                       Description of the api root (default: None)
 --default             Set as default api root (default: False)

examples.command: opentaxii-add-api-root: error: the following arguments are required: -t/--title

local_help.--help:
usage: opentaxii-add-api-root [-h] -t TITLE [-d DESCRIPTION] [--default]

Add a new taxii2 ApiRoot object.

options:
  -h, --help            show this help message and exit
  -t, --title TITLE     Title of the api root
  -d, --description DESCRIPTION
                        Description of the api root (default: None)
  --default             Set as default api root (default: False)
local_help.-h:
usage: opentaxii-add-api-root [-h] -t TITLE [-d DESCRIPTION] [--default]

Add a new taxii2 ApiRoot object.

options:
  -h, --help            show this help message and exit
  -t, --title TITLE     Title of the api root
  -d, --description DESCRIPTION
                        Description of the api root (default: None)
  --default             Set as default api root (default: False)

commands.raw_help:
root@kali:~# opentaxii-add-collection -h

                               [-a ALIAS] [--public] [--public-write]

 -r, --rootid {}       Api root id of the collection

arguments.description: show this help message and exit
arguments.raw: -h, --help            show this help message and exit
-c, --collection COLLECTION

arguments.description: delete inbox messages associated with deleted content
arguments.raw: -m, --with-messages   delete inbox messages associated with deleted content

arguments.description: exclusive beginning of time window as ISO8601
arguments.raw: --begin BEGIN         exclusive beginning of time window as ISO8601

arguments.description: inclusive ending of time window as ISO8601 formatted
arguments.raw: --end END             inclusive ending of time window as ISO8601 formatted

commands.raw_help:
root@kali:~# opentaxii-delete-blocks -h
 usage: opentaxii-delete-blocks [-h] -c COLLECTION [-m] --begin BEGIN
                              [--end END]
 
 Delete content blocks from specified collections with timestamp labels
 matching defined time window
 
 options:
 -h, --help            show this help message and exit
 -c, --collection COLLECTION
                       Collection to remove content blocks from
 -m, --with-messages   delete inbox messages associated with deleted content
                       blocks (default: False)
 --begin BEGIN         exclusive beginning of time window as ISO8601
                       formatted date
 --end END             inclusive ending of time window as ISO8601 formatted
                       date (default: None)

examples.command: opentaxii-delete-blocks: error: the following arguments are required: -c/--collection, --begin

local_help.--help:
usage: opentaxii-delete-blocks [-h] -c COLLECTION [-m] --begin BEGIN
                               [--end END]

Delete content blocks from specified collections with timestamp labels
matching defined time window

options:
  -h, --help            show this help message and exit
  -c, --collection COLLECTION
                        Collection to remove content blocks from
  -m, --with-messages   delete inbox messages associated with deleted content
                        blocks (default: False)
  --begin BEGIN         exclusive beginning of time window as ISO8601
                        formatted date
  --end END             inclusive ending of time window as ISO8601 formatted
                        date (default: None)
local_help.-h:
usage: opentaxii-delete-blocks [-h] -c COLLECTION [-m] --begin BEGIN
                               [--end END]

Delete content blocks from specified collections with timestamp labels
matching defined time window

commands.raw_help:
root@kali:~# opentaxii-sync-data -h
 usage: opentaxii-sync-data [-h] [-f] config
 
 Create services/collections/accounts
 
 positional arguments:
 config              YAML file with data configuration
 
 options:
 -h, --help          show this help message and exit
 -f, --force-delete  force deletion of collections and their content blocks
                     if collection is not defined in configuration file
                     (default: False)

  -f, --force-delete  force deletion of collections and their content blocks
                      if collection is not defined in configuration file
                      (default: False)

apt_metadata.depends: python3-blinker, python3-flask (>= 0.10.1), python3-jwt, python3-libtaxii, python3-lxml, python3-marshmallow, python3-mypy-extensions, python3-pytz, python3-six (>= 1.10.0), python3-sqlalchemy (>= 1.1.2), python3-stix2, python3-structlog, python3-yaml, python3:any
apt_metadata.description:
TAXII server implementation from EclecticIQ
This package contains a robust Python implementation of TAXII Services that
delivers rich feature set and friendly pythonic API built on top of well
designed application.
.
OpenTAXII is guaranteed to be compatible with Cabby, TAXII client library.

use_cases: When you need Kali Linux, use opentaxii-add-api-root because ### opentaxii.
When you need a concrete invocation, run `opentaxii-add-api-root [-h] -t TITLE [-d DESCRIPTION] [--default]` as documented upstream for opentaxii-add-api-root.
When chaining Kali Linux tools, pair opentaxii-add-api-root with sibling catalog entries sharing its attack_surface/phase_hint.
When validating lab setup, run `opentaxii-add-api-root --help` (or the README entrypoint) and confirm required hardware/deps from the catalog.

arguments.description: Option present in package usage line for opentaxii-add-api-root.

arguments.description: Option present in package usage line for opentaxii-add-api-root.

arguments.name: --default
arguments.description: Option present in package usage line for opentaxii-add-api-root.

documentation.how_to_use: Install/setup: sudo apt install opentaxii. Run the primary entrypoint as shown in upstream docs, e.g. `opentaxii-add-api-root [-h] -t TITLE [-d DESCRIPTION] [--default]`.
documentation.when_to_use: Use during the 'any' phase against surfaces [web] in the Kali Linux category. Fits when the engagement needs: ### opentaxii.
usage_sections: Install with: sudo apt install opentaxii
Install with: sudo apt install opentaxii
